commit.message:
Fix getStubConfig placeholder coords triggering real API calls and actions

getStubConfig() returned latitude: 47, longitude: -122 as example values.
When HA's card picker previews the card, it runs the full lifecycle with
that stub config - resolving coordinates to a real NWS zone, fetching live
alerts, and firing configured action scripts unintentionally.

Stub now returns only the minimum required config using nws_zone, avoiding
any coordinate resolution or live API calls during card preview.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/fix level progression bug mx6 bm (#345)

* Add Illness Log as dedicated tool and integrate into workout decisions

1. ExploreTab: Added Illness Log as its own tool in the Body category
   (was previously buried as a keyword under Injury Log). Full
   description surfaces the Neck Check protocol, daily check-ins, and
   return-to-training phases.

2. Daily Directive: Active illness now gates the "should I train today?"
   decision. If the illness engine says no training (fever, systemic,
   below-neck, or GI symptoms), the directive forces rest with
   illness-specific headline, actions, and training modifications.
   Recovering athletes get RPE caps and duration limits.

3. Performance Engine: Added illness as an 11th readiness factor
   (weight 0.10). Active illness scores 5/100 (triggers critical limiter
   cap), recovering scores proportional to allowed volume. Recently
   resolved illness (within 14 days) continues to impact readiness
   during return-to-training phases. Existing factor weights rebalanced
   to accommodate.

Science: Weidner 1998 Neck Check Rule, Friman & Wesslén 2000 fever/
myocarditis risk, Schwellnus 2016 return-to-play protocol.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix next workout progression skipping back to W1/D1

The getNextWorkout logic (daily directive) and nextUpSession (WorkoutView)
both iterated weeks from W1 forward, returning the first uncompleted
session. If a user completed a later session (e.g. Deload Day 1) but
earlier sessions were uncompleted, it would jump back to W1/D1 instead
of continuing to Deload Day 2.

Fix: find the most recently completed session by log date, then search
forward from that position for the next uncompleted session. Only falls
back to "first uncompleted from start" for fresh mesocycles with no
completions.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix mobile viewport overflow and pinch-to-zoom issues

- Lock viewport scale to 1 and disable user-scalable (standard PWA behavior)
- Remove max-w-lg from tab bar that constrained it unnecessarily
- Add overflow-x-hidden + w-full to main content container and overlay wrapper
- Change ExploreTab pinned tools from 4-col to 3-col grid for better mobile fit

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix nutrition tab ignoring user's grappling intensity selection

The duration threshold (>=60min) was overriding the user's explicit
intensity choice, so a light 75-min flow session got classified as
"hard grappling" with 1.2x calorie multiplier. Now only hard_sparring,
competition_prep, or long moderate sessions (90+ min) trigger hard
grappling nutrition. Light sessions stay light regardless of duration.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

* Fix HomeTab showing W1/D1 instead of next deload session

HomeTab's getNextWorkout() used naive first-incomplete-from-start logic,
while Program tab (WorkoutView) correctly scans forward from the most
recently completed session. Aligned HomeTab to use the same forward-scan
approach so both tabs show the same next workout.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01FracW8c5FPGAWk1NcmX4LQ

---------

commit.message:
Fix lesson 10: router labels must differ from method names to avoid infinite loop

@router returns labels ("approved", "needs_revision") instead of method
names ("finalize_article", "revise_draft") — matching label to own name
causes the method to re-trigger itself on every completion.

commit.message:
Fix bugs, harden security, and improve code quality

- Fix .tar.gz/.tar.xz stream decompression producing double-tar filenames
- Validate WAV header params (channels, sample_rate, bits_per_sample) to prevent panics on malformed files
- Add defense-in-depth path traversal check for ZIP extraction (starts_with)
- Sanitize config values on load (font_size, window dimensions)
- Reject Windows reserved device names (CON, PRN, etc.) in validate_name
- Deduplicate stream_decompress_output_name into file_ops.rs
- Derive Clone for AnimFrame, eliminating manual clone blocks
- Fix missing fields in test struct (panel.rs)
- Update CLAUDE.md with new design decisions and coding principles

commit.message:
Fix CronView "Rendered more hooks than during the previous render" error

Move useMemo hook above the early loading return so hooks are always
called in the same order on every render, satisfying React's Rules of Hooks.

Closes #48

commit.message:
Complete ingestion of Sales.csv into l10wrk_sales

- Created l10wrk_sales table with 62,884 transaction line items
- All rows successfully ingested from source
- Added to pipeline.yml
- Generated column statistics
- Created table documentation

Key findings:
- 26,326 distinct orders with average 2.16 line items per order
- 79.1% delivery_date null (likely in-store pickup or online orders)
- Data spans 2016-2021, peak in 2019 (21,611 rows)
- StoreKey=0 (13,165 rows, 20.9%) likely represents online/direct sales
- 5 currencies: USD dominant (53.7%), followed by EUR, GBP, CAD, AUD

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: implement true Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR)

Adds application-level WAL using PostgreSQL triggers to enable
recovery to any precise second, not just snapshot boundaries.

Migrations:
- 006_source_change_log.sql: change_log table + log_change() trigger
  function + triggers on all 25 core tables (run on SOURCE DB)
- 007_dr_pitr.sql: dr_change_log mirror + dr_pitr_checkpoints
  tracking table + dr_change_log_activity view (run on DR DB)

Backend:
- /api/cron/sync-changes: runs every 5 min, pulls new change_log
  entries from source DB and mirrors them into dr_change_log
- /api/restore/pitr: POST with target_timestamp, executes PITR restore
- /api/pitr/status: returns sync checkpoint info for UI banner
- src/lib/restore/pitr-executor.ts: core engine — finds base snapshot,
  restores it, then replays INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE entries in order
- vercel.json: added */5 * * * * cron for sync-changes

UI:
- PITRWizard.tsx: datetime picker, quick targets (15min/1h/6h/24h/3d/7d),
  sync status banner, confirm gate, progress bar, result summary
- RestoreTabs.tsx: tab switcher between Snapshot Restore and PITR
- restore/page.tsx: updated to use RestoreTabs, PITR badge in history

commit.message:
Complete ingestion of Customers.csv into l10wrk_customers

- Created l10wrk_customers table with 15,266 rows (all rows from source)
- Fixed encoding issue: source file was latin-1, converted to UTF-8
- Added to pipeline.yml
- Generated column statistics
- Created table documentation

Encoding resolution:
- Source file contained German city names with umlauts (Fürstenberg, Düsseldorf, etc.)
- Initially attempted DuckDB native encodings (IBM_1252) but had header parsing issues
- Solution: Read with Python latin-1 encoding, write UTF-8 temp file, ingest via DuckDB
- All 15,266 rows successfully ingested including 1,473 German customer records

commit.message:
Filter builder containers from list/cleanup commands

Builder containers (temporary containers from image builds) could linger
after interrupted builds, showing confusing "check failed" in cleanup output.
Now: cleanup auto-removes stale builders, list hides them. Detection uses
known image names only (not bare suffix match) to avoid false positives on
custom-named bubbles. Running builders are skipped to avoid killing active
builds.

- Session list sorted: running > queued > idle > stopped, then by last_run_at desc

- Provider.complete() abstract interface gains on_text_chunk kwarg (None default)

commit.message:
fix: start polling in simulated mode so simulator UI shows activity

The polling loop that fetches simulator state, level maps, trades, and
diagnostics was gated on Tradovate connection only. When running on
simulated data without Tradovate, the UI never polled — so the simulator
appeared inactive despite running in the background.

- Start polling on mount if shadow trading is enabled (even without Tradovate)
- Start polling when shadow toggle is turned on
- Restart polling on symbol change in simulated mode
- Stop polling when shadow is disabled and Tradovate is not connected
- Bump version to v1.17.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add lesson 09: human-in-the-loop with dual review gates

Demonstrates human_input=True on tasks — crew pauses after agent
drafts output, collects human feedback, agent refines and continues.
Two-gate pattern: draft review + final editorial approval.

commit.message:
feat(prism-cli): decision + question checkpoint system

When a prompt signals a required decision ("decide between", "trade-offs",
etc.), the agent now fires a one-shot nudge after 8 read-only tool calls
prompting it to present options with trade-offs before implementing.
A periodic question checkpoint fires every 5 turns to surface ambiguities.

Both thresholds are env-overridable via PRISM_DECISION_CHECKPOINT_THRESHOLD
and PRISM_QUESTION_CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL. Threshold reads are cached once per
run to avoid repeated env::var calls in the turn loop.

System prompt softened to allow pausing for ambiguity and approach
decisions, with added guidelines on derive-vs-ask judgment.

commit.message:
feat(prism-cli): disciplined exploration protocol + budget nudge

Replace flat Guidelines with a structured Exploration Protocol in the
system prompt: parse requirements first, targeted search (3-8 calls),
then propose before implementing. Explicit anti-patterns call out
directory browsing and reading entire modules for context.

Add ExplorationBudget to track consecutive read-only turns. After a
configurable threshold (default 4, PRISM_EXPLORATION_NUDGE_TURNS=0 to
disable), inject a convergence nudge asking the agent to summarize
findings or state what is blocking it. The streak resets on any write
tool use or substantive text output (>100 trimmed chars).

Fixes in simplify pass:
- Rename nudged → streak_nudged (clarifies per-streak scope)
- Treat ToolOutcome::Denied as non-read-only (denied action ≠ passive read)
- Trim whitespace before the 100-char substantive-text check
- Use self.threshold in nudge message (not the coincidentally equal counter)

fix(zed): use menu.action() for Open PrisM Dashboard to fix dispatch

custom_entry ignores the opener's FocusHandle, so when dispatch_action
defers execution the popover node is already gone from the rendered frame
and the action falls back to root (missing the workspace handler). Using
menu.action() refocuses the opener's context before dispatching, ensuring
ToggleFocus reaches the workspace action handler.

commit.message:
feat(prism-cli): post-turn compile validation + ACP display fix

Adds compile_check module that runs a configurable shell command
(PRISM_COMPILE_CHECK) after any turn that touches code files, injecting
the result into the conversation so the agent self-corrects on build
failures without human intervention.

- compile_check.rs: new module with code-extension filter and
  run_compile_check() — caps timeout at 300s, single UTF-8 conversion
  path (prefer stderr, fall back to stdout)
- config.rs: compile_check_command + compile_check_timeout fields
  read from PRISM_COMPILE_CHECK / PRISM_COMPILE_CHECK_TIMEOUT
- agent/mod.rs: per-turn turn_files tracking; inject compile result
  as user message + renderer display after skill injection
- acp.rs: same turn_files tracking; surface result via send_text_chunk
  (so user sees it in real-time) before injecting into LLM context
- render.rs: compile_check() renderer method (green=pass, yellow=fail)

Also fixes:
- common.rs: unescaped inner quotes in SYSTEM_PROMPT constant broke
  Rust string parsing
- agent/mod.rs: spurious `ref` binding modifier in match on borrowed
  ToolOutcome removed per compiler suggestion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

チャンク30-60文字に縮小+synthesis詳細ログ+50sタイムアウト追加

- チャンクサイズ: 40-80 → 30-60文字（上限80）で1GB VPSメモリ対策
- synthesis fetch前後に詳細ログ（開始/応答時間/bodyサイズ）
- fetch失敗時にエラーオブジェクト全体(name,message,cause)をログ出力
- AbortSignal.timeout(50000)でハング防止
- エラーレスポンスにchunk_index, chunk_lengthを追加

commit.message:
Fix model loading: switch pickle to joblib

Model is saved with joblib.dump() in training notebook; pickle.load()
fails with STACK_GLOBAL error. Use joblib.load() for compatibility.
